published_prior_art.cite: Hahn1998Econometrica and HiranoImbensRidder2003Econometrica establish classical unconfounded-ATE efficiency; DML/ADML sources restate the same EIF baseline

repo_prior_art.cite: closest is eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1, but that was a weak-overlap studentization frontier, not a fixed-law LRO weighted-GMM Riesz iff

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO, https://doi.org/10.3982/ECTA16294, gives generic debiased GMM influence/sandwich formulas but not this ATE-specific normalization iff

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1: primitive two-cell witness for strict separation is close, but used weak-overlap triangular-array studentization rather than this fixed two-cell residual-loading check

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the survey misses IchimuraNewey2022QE, https://ideas.repec.org/a/wly/quante/v13y2022i1p29-61.html, and the current kernel is a generic IF-equality algebra unless tied to specific published LRO normalizations with a strict frontier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Upgrade Conjecture 1 from an arbitrary-J algebraic classification to a frontier for one or two named LRO normalizations actually used or recommended in ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO, with explicit alpha_J formulas and a complementary strict non-equivalence regime.

Add Ichimura-Newey 2022 and overidentified-GMM efficiency literature to Related work, then state exactly why the proposed ATE frontier is not just the standard GMM influence-function row specialized to a scalar parameter.
Put the Diagnostic 1 cell arithmetic in Section 9 explicitly, including K=11/2, C=9/2, and Delta=7/2, so the noncancellation reduction is auditable without prose reconstruction.

recommended_next_step: Revise the flagship kernel around specific published LRO normalizations or downgrade the target tier; the current proposal is coherent but not flagship-level yet.
